prompt: Create a cinematic ultra-detailed wide-angle scene of an abandoned Victorian industrial greenhouse power station reclaimed by nature, with rusted iron beams, broken glass walls, a high arched glass-and-steel ceiling, hanging ivy, moss, puddles, corroded pipes, turbine-like machinery, and a long dark stone walkway leading toward a bright circular window at the far end. In the left foreground, place a decaying metal control console covered in rust and moss, topped by one ornate antique mantel clock with a yellowed Roman-numeral face and elaborate gold-brown filigree. Show exactly 7 visible analog time-and-pressure instruments in the foreground: 1 ornate mantel clock on top, 5 small round gauges embedded across the sloped console face, and 1 lower round gauge hanging near the central lever mechanism. Add old brass levers, valves, empty circular ports, cracked glass, verdigris, and heavy patina. The mood is rusted decadence, melancholy beauty, time reclaiming industry. Use warm golden-hour sunlight streaming through the broken roof and tall windows, with dramatic volumetric rays, floating dust, deep green shadows, amber highlights, wet reflective floor stones, and high contrast. Make the composition highly immersive and photorealistic, like a dystopian steampunk ruin after decades of abandonment, with no people, no modern objects, no readable text, no watermark. Use a 16:9 landscape aspect ratio, sharp foreground detail, rich texture, cinematic color grading, and atmospheric depth.
